table_description: Contains customer (user, client, account holder) profile details such as name, email, phone, and geographic information. Used to answer queries related to customer details, user profiles, premium users, and customer demographics. Each customer is uniquely identified by customer_id. This table is commonly joined with orders, subscriptions, support_tickets, and reviews using customer_id. Frequently filtered using country, city, created_at, and is_premium status.
create_statement: CREATE TABLE customers (customer_id STRING, name STRING, email STRING, phone STRING, created_at TIMESTAMP, country STRING, city STRING, is_premium BOOL);

columns.name: customer_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for each customer (Primary Key)

columns.name: email
columns.description: Email address used for communication and login

columns.name: created_at
columns.description: Timestamp when the customer account was created

columns.name: is_premium
columns.description: Indicates whether the customer is a premium subscriber

table_description: Stores purchase order (transaction, sale) information made by customers. Used for queries involving order history, purchases, sales trends, and revenue analysis. Each order is uniquely identified by order_id and linked to customers using customer_id. Frequently joined with order_items, payments, shipments, and returns. Common filters include order_date, order_status, and total_amount.
create_statement: CREATE TABLE orders (order_id STRING, customer_id STRING, order_date TIMESTAMP, order_status STRING, total_amount FLOAT64);

columns.name: order_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for each order (Primary Key)

columns.name: customer_id
columns.description: Reference to the customer placing the order

columns.name: order_date
columns.description: Date and time when the order was placed

columns.name: order_status
columns.description: Current status of the order (e.g., shipped, delivered, cancelled)

columns.name: total_amount
columns.description: Total monetary value of the order

table_description: Represents individual items (products) within each order. Used for queries involving product-level sales, quantities sold, and itemized order breakdowns. Each row links an order to a product using order_id and product_id. Frequently joined with orders and products. Important for calculating revenue and product performance metrics.
create_statement: CREATE TABLE order_items (order_item_id STRING, order_id STRING, product_id STRING, quantity INT64, price_per_unit FLOAT64);

columns.name: order_item_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for each order item

columns.name: price_per_unit
columns.description: Price of a single unit at purchase time

table_description: Contains product (item, goods) catalog details including name, pricing, and category. Used for queries related to product listings, pricing, and catalog management. Each product is uniquely identified by product_id and linked to categories. Frequently joined with order_items, reviews, and inventory.
create_statement: CREATE TABLE products (product_id STRING, name STRING, category_id STRING, price FLOAT64, is_active BOOL);

columns.name: is_active
columns.description: Indicates if the product is currently available

table_description: Stores product category hierarchy used to group similar products. Enables queries on product categorization and grouping. Each category has a unique category_id and may reference a parent category. Frequently joined with products.
create_statement: CREATE TABLE categories (category_id STRING, category_name STRING, parent_category_id STRING);

table_description: Stores payment transaction details associated with orders. Used for financial queries such as successful payments, failed transactions, and revenue tracking. Each payment is linked to an order using order_id. Frequently filtered by payment_status, payment_method, and payment_date.
create_statement: CREATE TABLE payments (payment_id STRING, order_id STRING, payment_method STRING, payment_status STRING, amount FLOAT64, payment_date TIMESTAMP);

columns.name: payment_method
columns.description: Method used for payment (card, UPI, etc.)

table_description: Tracks shipping and delivery details for orders. Used to analyze delivery performance, shipping delays, and logistics. Each shipment is linked to an order using order_id. Frequently filtered by shipment_status, carrier, and delivery dates.
create_statement: CREATE TABLE shipments (shipment_id STRING, order_id STRING, shipment_status STRING, shipped_date TIMESTAMP, delivered_date TIMESTAMP, carrier STRING);

table_description: Stores customer feedback and product reviews including ratings and comments. Used to analyze customer satisfaction and product quality. Each review links a customer to a product. Frequently filtered by rating and product_id.
create_statement: CREATE TABLE reviews (review_id STRING, product_id STRING, customer_id STRING, rating INT64, review_text STRING);

table_description: Tracks customer subscription details for recurring services. Used to analyze active users, churn, and subscription revenue. Linked to customers via customer_id and plans via plan_id.
create_statement: CREATE TABLE subscriptions (subscription_id STRING, customer_id STRING, plan_id STRING, start_date TIMESTAMP, end_date TIMESTAMP, status STRING);

table_description: Defines subscription plans including pricing and billing cycles. Used to understand subscription tiers and pricing models.
create_statement: CREATE TABLE subscription_plans (plan_id STRING, plan_name STRING, monthly_cost FLOAT64, billing_cycle STRING);

table_description: Stores customer support issues and complaints. Used to track customer issues, resolution time, and service quality. Linked to customers and optionally to orders.
create_statement: CREATE TABLE support_tickets (ticket_id STRING, customer_id STRING, order_id STRING, issue_type STRING, status STRING, created_at TIMESTAMP);

table_description: Stores warehouse locations and capacities used for inventory storage. Helps in logistics and stock distribution queries.
create_statement: CREATE TABLE warehouses (warehouse_id STRING, location STRING, capacity INT64);

table_description: Tracks stock levels of products across warehouses. Used for inventory management, stock availability, and supply chain analysis. Links products and warehouses.
create_statement: CREATE TABLE inventory (inventory_id STRING, product_id STRING, warehouse_id STRING, stock_quantity INT64);
